notes: Most widely used clinical standard. Targets 140kDa adhesion protein. Sensitivity <5 copies/reaction. Note: MgPa has variable regions — some strain variants possible but risk is low for qPCR.

reference: Baczynska et al. 2004 JCM 42:3260; multiple validated sets
notes: Species-specific 16S primers. 81% prevalence in HIV+ SA pregnant women. BV synergy. HIV shedding association.

reference: Kong et al. 2000 JCM 38:1175; Yi et al. 2005
notes: Distinguishes U. parvum from U. urealyticum by ureC sequence. Dominant Ureaplasma species in vaginal samples. 77% in SA HIV+ women.

reference: Kong et al. 2000 JCM 38:1175; Yi et al. 2005
notes: Same forward/reverse primers as U. parvum; species discrimination by TaqMan probe sequence. Less common but more pathogenic.
